select_when: A forecast, estimate, or recommendation is about to determine commitment size, but the confidence behind it has not been made explicit.
The team is speaking with precise-sounding certainty even though the evidence base, sample depth, or regime stability is still weak.
A branch model, dashboard, or spreadsheet looks decision-grade, but the real question is how much trust the underlying measurement has earned.
The loudest or most experienced voice may be directionally right, but the cost of untested overconfidence is high enough to justify explicit boundary conditions.
avoid_when: The model is being used to add ranges and caveats without naming the action threshold that would still trigger a real decision.
Percentages or confidence intervals are being presented as if quantified language itself proved disciplined judgment.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on earned confidence, boundary conditions, and instrument-trust checks rather than on generic humility language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style forecasting overlays because the raw source is already explicit on ranges, confidence measures, and instrument trust.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stays inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The typed labels compress a source that spans self-assessment, forecasting, and organizational decision hygiene.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that calibration matters most when commitment size, branch models, and expert certainty are outrunning ground truth.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions come directly from the source danger lines on caveat theater and quantified language without earned judgment.

input_type.note: The source activates on forecasts and recommendations that size commitment; the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The source produces explicit confidence ranges and boundary conditions; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Confidence calibration is clearly metacognitive and probabilistic in use, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
